name: Chaim Weizmann
bio: Zionist leader, chemist, diplomat, first President of Israel, and key architect of the Balfour Declaration.

name: Heinrich Heine
bio: German Jewish poet, journalist, and literary critic, known for his lyric poetry and essays.

name: Sir Moses Haim Montefiore
bio: British financier, philanthropist, activist, Sheriff of London, and leading Jewish communal figure. President of the Board of Deputies of British Jews (1835–1874). A pivotal figure in Proto-Zionism; founded Mishkenot Sha'ananim (1860), the first Jewish neighborhood outside Jerusalem’s Old City.

name: Solomon Schechter
bio: Scholar and rabbinic leader. Famous for his work on the Cairo Geniza and as the second President of the Jewish Theological Seminary of America.

name: Theodor Herzl
bio: Theodor Herzl (1860–1904) was an Austro-Hungarian journalist, playwright, and political activist, widely regarded as the father of modern political Zionism. Trained in law and active as a correspondent and writer, he became convinced that the Jewish people could only ensure their security and dignity through the establishment of a sovereign Jewish state. His pamphlet 'Der Judenstaat' (1896) laid out this vision, and he went on to organize the First Zionist Congress in Basel in 1897, becoming the first President of the World Zionist Organization. Herzl tirelessly lobbied European leaders and Ottoman officials for support until his death in 1904.
